patterns.title: Summarisation / Extraction
patterns.command: bar build pull gist struct --subject "..."
patterns.example: bar build pull gist struct --subject "[long RFC or design document]"
patterns.desc: Use when compressing a long source document into a shorter summary. Prefer pull over show when a SUBJECT document is being compressed: pull extracts a subset, show explains a concept. Heuristic: long SUBJECT to compress → pull; concept to explain without a source → show.

patterns.title: Test Coverage Gap Analysis
patterns.command: bar build check fail full checklist --subject "..."
patterns.example: bar build check fail full checklist --subject "Feature: user registration flow"
patterns.desc: Use when identifying missing tests or coverage gaps in existing code. Heuristic: 'what tests are missing?' → check; 'write a test plan' → make.

patterns.title: Test Plan Creation
patterns.command: bar build make act fail full checklist --subject "..."
patterns.example: bar build make act fail full checklist --subject "Payment integration feature"
patterns.desc: Use when creating a new test plan or test cases from scratch. Produces a test plan artifact rather than evaluating existing coverage.

patterns.title: Pre-mortem / Inversion Exercise
patterns.command: bar build probe fail full inversion variants --subject "..."
patterns.example: bar build probe fail full inversion variants --subject "Our Q4 launch plan"
patterns.desc: Use when assuming failure and working backward to identify causes. Frames the exercise as: 'assume this has failed — what went wrong?' Pairs naturally with planning and architecture review tasks.

patterns.title: Comprehensive Assessment (Multi-Scope)
patterns.command: bar build check <scope> full <method> --subject "..."
patterns.example: bar build check good full analysis --subject "Assess codebase quality"
patterns.desc: Use for multi-faceted assessments that span quality (good), fragility (fail), and structure (struct). When the task requires multiple analytical lenses, prioritize by primary concern or analyze sequentially: quality-first (good), risk-first (fail), or architecture-first (struct).

patterns.title: Evaluation with Falsification
patterns.command: bar build check fail full verify --subject "..."
patterns.example: bar build check fail full verify --subject "Evaluate the proposed caching strategy"
patterns.desc: Use when evaluating claims by actively searching for ways they could be wrong. Combines verify (falsification pressure) with scope=fail (failure modes and fragility). Best for: reviewing designs, validating assumptions, stress-testing proposals.

patterns.title: Plain Prose Output
patterns.command: bar build show <scope> full plain --subject "..."
patterns.example: bar build show mean full plain --subject "Explain the authorization model"
patterns.desc: Use when the response must be plain prose — no lists, bullets, or tables. The plain channel explicitly suppresses structural decoration. Heuristic: 'no bullets', 'no formatting', 'plain prose', 'flowing paragraphs' → add plain channel to any task.

patterns.title: Synchronous Session Plan
patterns.command: bar build plan act full sync --subject "..."
patterns.example: bar build plan act full sync --subject "Design sprint kickoff — 3h with context, problem framing, and ideation"
patterns.desc: Use when the output should be a synchronous session plan with agenda, timing slots, and facilitation cues for real-time use. Heuristic: 'session plan', 'live workshop', 'meeting agenda with timing', 'facilitation script for live session' → sync channel. Combine with facilitate form when facilitator role is explicit.

patterns.title: Handoff / Continuation
patterns.command: bar build make relay full --subject "..."
patterns.example: bar build make relay full --subject "Design the API contract for the payment service"
patterns.desc: Use when producing an artifact another person or agent will continue from without prior context

debug-cycle.description: Surface root causes, fix them, then verify the fix holds.
debug-cycle.example: A production service is returning 500s intermittently and the cause is unknown.
heuristics: find the bug fix it check it

steps.prompt_hint: Use this step to surface hidden assumptions and possible causes before writing any fix.
Use this step to implement the targeted fix once the root cause is confirmed.
Use this step to verify the fix holds and no regressions were introduced.

experiment-cycle.description: Frame a hypothesis before running an experiment, then review evidence afterward.
experiment-cycle.example: Testing whether adding a cache layer reduces p95 latency below 200ms.
experiment-cycle.stop_when: The hypothesis has been confirmed or falsified — further iterations would not change the conclusion.

steps.prompt_hint: Use this step to state the hypothesis and success criteria before running the experiment.
Run the experiment defined in the prior step. Collect results before proceeding to vet.
Use this step to evaluate the evidence against the original hypothesis.

extract-and-package.description: Extract a relevant subset, then package it for downstream use.
extract-and-package.example: Pull the authentication flow from a large codebase and package it as context for a new engineer.

steps.prompt_hint: Use this step to identify and extract only the relevant material from the subject.
Use this step to wrap the extracted material with enough context for a downstream reader or LLM.

gather-and-synthesize.example: Understanding why engineers on a team are reluctant to write tests.

steps.prompt_hint: Use this step to produce interview questions, a research plan, or observation vectors the user can go execute.
Use this step to extract patterns and generalize conclusions from the gathered findings.

check-and-rewrite.description: Evaluate an artifact against criteria, then rewrite it so the gaps are closed.
check-and-rewrite.example: Reviewing a technical design doc and rewriting the sections that don't hold up.

steps.prompt_hint: Use this step to evaluate the subject against criteria and surface specific gaps.
Use this step to rewrite the artifact so that the gaps identified in the evaluation are closed — change presentation or structure, not meaning.

make-and-review.description: Produce an artifact, then review it critically before treating it as done.
make-and-review.example: Drafting an ADR and reviewing it for gaps before publishing.

steps.prompt_hint: Use this step to produce the artifact — document, plan, design, or code.
Use this step to review the artifact: what was produced, how it compares to intent, what is missing or weak.

probe-and-plan.description: Diagnose the structure and hidden assumptions of a situation, then derive a targeted action plan.
probe-and-plan.example: Figuring out why a team is slow to ship, then planning what to change.

steps.prompt_hint: Use this step to surface hidden assumptions, root causes, and structural factors before planning.

plan-and-retrospect.example: Improving a team's code review process over two iterations.

steps.prompt_hint: Use this step to produce concrete steps the user can go implement.
Use this step to evaluate what worked, what didn't, and what to adjust in the next cycle.

scenario-to-plan.description: Simulate a scenario, then derive the action plan it implies.

Use this step to convert the simulation's implications into a concrete action plan.
